title: malva officinalis
intro: Malva officinalis, also known as marsh mallow, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, belonging to the family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, soothing, and emollient effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Malva officinalis include relief from irritable bowel syndrome, digestive disorders, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Malva officinalis include teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to make syrups, salves, and creams for topical application, as well as capsules or tablets for oral consumption.
